enrichment.summary: Kyle gives a vlog update from Boulder, covering his switch to Adobe Creative Cloud, his Google Earth project recreating the ghost town of Caribou, Colorado (a mining boomtown near Boulder active ca. 1869–1905), and a lengthy digression on mountain lion population, historical encounters with lions, and how to defend yourself against a lion attack while mountain biking at night.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: If a lion kills a person, kill 20 lions to reestablish human dominance; Boulder has gone too soft on wildlife
Attacks will increase as more people do night mountain biking; people need a plan and should be prepared to fight back
Recreating Caribou on Google Earth building-by-building is a valuable art and history project worth pursuing
Justified the cost because of the full suite and included tech support
Boulder is more physically wild now than ancient Europe was in its time; mountain lions and bears are thriving

entities: Arion (musician saved by dolphins, Greek myth)

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle is doing a request video vlog update because Love for Nature gave him a request.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle was working on his computer when it became distracting due to a window prompting him to download Lion.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle downloaded Lion but found that Dreamweaver couldn't work on his system, forcing him to upgrade.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle purchased Dreamweaver CS5 in January 2011 but now has to pay monthly installments for Creative Cloud.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle got the entire Creative Cloud package for $5 a month through a deal for developers.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle plans to watch tutorials and use Creative Cloud for web design and video editing.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle’s internet is slow, and it took a week to download and install Creative Cloud programs.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle couldn’t type in Dreamweaver and hasn’t called tech support yet, but he gets free tech support with the monthly plan.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle plans to upload his new K-Lin and Galer files now that he’s doing a video.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle started working on Google Earth projects again after Dreamweaver stopped working.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle mapped all ski resorts in Colorado, including runs and extended resorts like Silver Boom.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle discovered ski resorts in Colorado with 1800s train services for wealthy Europeans.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle feels like an outsider in Colorado despite living there for a long time.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle decided to map all the ghost towns in Colorado, including Caribou.

claims.subject: Caribou
claims.claim: Caribou, Colorado, was a ghost town that started in 1869 and burned down in 1905.

claims.subject: Caribou
claims.claim: Caribou had up to 3,000 people at its peak and was the highest town in America.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle is recreating Caribou on Google Earth using pictures and panoramic views.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle plans to label all buildings in Caribou and populate it with 3,000 virtual residents.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle is using Google Earth to visualize towns and compare them to ancient settlements.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle believes seeing the development of the earth from beginning to end is the highest form of knowledge.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle made a map of Boulder County showing ghost towns and mining sites.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle thinks Boulder County is more wild now than it was 2000 years ago due to increased wildlife.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle believes mountain lion attacks will increase as people become more adventurous in the mountains.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle plans to create a map for endurance bikers showing ghost towns and areas with lions and bears.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle wants to research how to fight a mountain lion and recommends carrying mace and blades.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle thinks wearing an inflated stuff sack while biking can prevent mountain lions from getting a grip on your neck.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle believes mountain lions should be killed if they attack humans to reduce their population.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle read a story about a man in Africa who fought off a lion with a knife and survived.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle thinks people should be prepared for mountain lion attacks and not be scared.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle believes Boulder County has too many bears and mountain lions due to increased deer populations.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle plans to create a page on how to fight mountain lions for endurance bikers.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle thinks mountain lions are more dangerous now because people are less aggressive toward them.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle believes mountain lions will attack humans if they are hungry or feel threatened.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle thinks Boulder County’s wildlife is more abundant now than in the past due to conservation efforts.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle plans to continue working on his Caribou project and upload it for others to see.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle wants to recreate Caribou with accurate statistics and virtual residents to bring the town back to life.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle thinks recreating Caribou on Google Earth is a cool art project that combines history and technology.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle believes Caribou was a highly civilized town built by engineers and miners with significant resources.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle plans to explore Colorado’s ghost towns and mining sites on his motorcycle.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle thinks Boulder County’s ghost towns are fascinating and worth exploring.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle believes Boulder County’s history is rich with mining and ghost towns that are often overlooked.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle plans to write about his explorations of Colorado’s ghost towns and mining sites.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle thinks Boulder County’s wildlife is more diverse now than in the past due to conservation efforts.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle believes mountain lions are a significant part of Boulder County’s ecosystem and history.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle thinks Boulder County’s wildlife is more abundant now than in the past due to increased conservation efforts.

claims.claim: Kyle plans to continue working on his Caribou project and upload it for others to see.
